projects.title: Migrate Flask backend to culinary-ops
projects.description: Replace Google Apps Script + Flask backend in betterday-app with culinary-ops NestJS endpoints, phase by phase. Goal: retire GAS entirely so all corporate data lives in PostgreSQL.

subtasks.title: Build corp manager + portal API endpoints in culinary-ops
subtasks.notes: ~25 endpoints. nest build passes. Awaiting Gurleen's review.

subtasks.title: Smoke-test PR #7 endpoints with a real corp_manager JWT
subtasks.notes: Verify cross-company access is blocked, bulk replace transactions roll back, par-cart rebuild works against a seeded company.

subtasks.title: Phase 1 — Switch /manager/dashboard read-only routes to culinary-ops
subtasks.notes: Lowest risk. Replace _gas_post() calls with requests.get() against culinary-ops, with GAS fallback for 1 week.

subtasks.title: Phase 2 — Replace GAS magic-token + PIN flows with culinary-ops JWT auth
subtasks.notes: Manager auth model changes from magic-link to PIN-only. Store JWT in Flask session.

subtasks.title: Phase 3 — Rewire employee + benefit-level CRUD to culinary-ops
subtasks.notes: All endpoints already exist in PR #7. Frontend needs to switch from GAS actions to /api/corp-manager/* calls.

subtasks.title: Phase 4 — Migrate order submission + par level write paths
subtasks.notes: Highest-risk phase — directly affects what food gets produced. Dual-write GAS + culinary-ops for at least a week before cutover.

subtasks.title: Phase 5 — Build invoicing + financials pipeline in culinary-ops
subtasks.notes: Endpoints exist in PR #7. Needs frontend wiring for invoice generation, credit notes, AR summary.

subtasks.title: Phase 6 — Retire GAS: remove _gas_post(), archive backend.gs, drop buffer sheet
subtasks.notes: Final cleanup. Remove GOOGLE_SCRIPT_URL env var. All corporate data lives in PostgreSQL after this.

projects.title: Manager Dashboard rebuild — propaganda + functional
projects.description: Rebuild the BetterDay For Work manager dashboard Overview tab from a passive 3-card grid into an action-oriented control panel. Replaces 'haven't ordered' name list with team favorite + avg-meals + company/BD subsidy stats; replaces vague Par Order card with a true micro-invoice (subtotal → volume discount → BD contribution → Company pays); adds week-by-week month spend table with optional soft budget tracker, top-eaters leaderboard, ROI calculator (hours × rate vs. program spend), coming-soon menu preview placeholder, and sidebar status dots. Lives in betterday-app/templates/manager_dashboard.html (Flask Jinja2). No backend changes for Sprints 1-4; Sprint 5 (Monthly Budget System) adds 3 fields to the company table and an alerts cron.

subtasks.title: Sprint 1 — Rebuild Overview action bar (Employee Orders box + Office Par micro-invoice + If-you-do-nothing note)
subtasks.notes: Employee Orders box: drop name list, add avg-meals/employee + company subsidy + BD contribution stat strip + team favorite (thumbnail+name) + quick-add link. Par box: replace single-number status with categorized item list (no per-category prices) + Subtotal/Discount/BD/Company-pays grand total. Add dashed do-nothing note clarifying Wed-midnight cutoff for Mon delivery. CSS done, HTML done, JS for favorite/top-users + par-mini-invoice still pending.

subtasks.title: Sprint 2 — Replace 'This Week at a Glance' 3-card grid with 'This Month at a Glance' 2-card layout
subtasks.notes: Card A: week-by-week spend table for the active month (rows from sorted_weeks filtered by anchor[:7] == sorted_monthly[0].key) + sub-stat pills (meals, $/employee, top meal). Card B: big participation %, 3-cell mini bench (this week %, active team, avg meals/emp), top-3 eaters leaderboard, link to full Employees report. Old 3-card grid + Financials column deleted (financials surface in sidebar status dots + Card A pills). HTML done, top-users JS pending.

subtasks.title: Sprint 3 — Impact strip + ROI calculator (replaces 'share with leadership' button)
subtasks.notes: Navy gradient strip with 3 lifetime stats (meals delivered, hours reclaimed at ½ hr/meal, BD covered) + interactive ROI calc: hours × adjustable $/hr input → productivity recovered → net ROI vs. company program spend. Kills the 'lunch runs avoided' fake stat. HTML done, updateRoiCalc() JS wireup pending.

subtasks.title: Sprint 4 — Coming-soon menu preview card + sidebar status dots + quick-add stub
subtasks.notes: Navy gradient placeholder card teasing next week's menu preview (build comes later). Red dot on Invoices when outstanding > 0, yellow dot on Orders when not_ordered > 0, yellow dot on Office Par Levels when editing window open. Quick-add employee button is a stub modal — no /manager/add-employee Flask route exists yet.

subtasks.title: Sprint 4 — JS plumbing: computeFavoriteAndTopUsers + updateParOverviewCards rewrite + updateRoiCalc + openQuickAddEmployee
subtasks.notes: All four use existing client-side ORDERS array (no backend changes). Favorite/top-users iterates ORDERS by current month, ranks by meal count. updateParOverviewCards rewrite groups PL_CATEGORIES by name into the new #parMiniInvoice element with subtotal → discount → BD → Company-pays lines.

subtasks.title: Sprint 5 — Monthly Budget System (soft cap, company spend only, no BD)
subtasks.notes: Add monthly_budget_amount + monthly_budget_set_at + monthly_budget_set_by to company table. Single input in Account Settings (soft cap only — no hard cutoff). Wire progress bar into Card A .mcn-budget block with green/yellow/red states at 75%/90%/100% of company_covered (BD contribution excluded). Pacing forecast = current_month_spend × (days_in_month / days_elapsed). Email alerts fire once per month per threshold.

subtasks.title: Validation pass — read-back all template edits, syntax-check Jinja + JS
subtasks.notes: No preview server per session rule. Verify Jinja braces balance, action-bar grid renders without parlevels_on, ORDERS-derived JS handles empty array, do-nothing-note copy correct, sidebar dots only render when conditions match. Flask app must boot clean.

branches.title: Corporate manager + portal API endpoints
branches.summary: ~25 new endpoints covering manager CRUD (employees, benefit levels, par levels, company self-service, PIN), portal order counts and item swaps, and BD admin invoicing/credit notes/AR. No schema changes.
what_works: All routes scoped to req.user.company_id with cross-company access blocked
Bulk replace endpoints use $transaction so partial failures roll back
what_needs_review: Smoke test endpoints with a real corp_manager JWT
Confirm getCompanyPin UX change for the manager dashboard (bcrypt PINs cannot be retrieved)
Decide whether to split CorpAdminService into Manager + BD Admin services

tasks.title: Review PR #7 (feature/corp-manager-api)
tasks.notes: First PR from Conner's side. Build passes clean. Test plan checklist is in the PR body. Patterns followed your existing CorpAdminService structure — would love a sanity check before merging.

tasks.title: After PR #7 merges, plan Phase 1 of Flask migration (read-only routes)
tasks.notes: First step: switch /manager/dashboard in betterday-app to call culinary-ops endpoints instead of GAS. See GAS_to_CulinaryOps_Integration_Map.md on Conner's desktop for the full migration plan.

messages.subject: First PR from my side: corp manager API endpoints
messages.body:
Hey Gurleen! Just opened PR #7 in culinary-ops with the corporate manager + portal endpoints we needed for migrating off Google Apps Script. ~25 routes, no schema changes, nest build passes clean.

When you have a moment, please take a look — would love your review on the patterns I followed since this is the first non-Gurleen PR in the repo. Test plan checklist is in the PR body.

Also: this collab board is new! It's how we'll coordinate from here on. You can ask your Claude session to update it for you, or edit data.json directly via the GitHub web UI.

messages.subject: How to use the collab board (+ Claude protocol)
messages.body:
Hey Gurleen — quick how-to-use guide for the board now that the structure is filled out a bit more.

**Live site:** https://betterday-foodco.github.io/collab/

**The four tabs at the top:**

1. **Inbox** — anything that needs YOUR action: unread messages, subtasks assigned to you with all dependencies satisfied, open tasks, branches you own. Empty = caught up. (Right now you have task-001 here: review PR #7.)

2. **Projects** — big initiatives grouped by status (Active / Blocked / Paused / Done). Click any project card to see its subtasks plus all linked branches/tasks/messages inline in one place. Done subtasks auto-collapse into a 'done' pill — click to expand. Right now there's one project: 'Migrate Flask backend to culinary-ops' with 8 phases mapped out so you can see where this whole thing is heading.

3. **Activity** — chronological feed of every event (project created, PR opened, task created, message sent, subtask completed). Use this to scan 'what happened recently?'

4. **Archive** — done projects, done tasks, merged/closed PRs. Out of sight but findable.

There's a **View as: Everyone / Conner / Gurleen** filter at the top right that filters everything to one person's perspective. Try 'Gurleen' for your personalized view.

**The cool part — talking to it through Claude:**

You don't have to hand-edit data.json. The repo has a file called `HOW_TO_USE.md` that's written specifically FOR Claude Code. When you tell your Claude session something like:

- 'check the collab board'
- 'any new messages?'
- 'send Conner a message saying X'
- 'add a task for me to review PR #8'
- 'mark task-001 as done'

…Claude pulls latest, reads data.json, follows the protocol in HOW_TO_USE.md, and pushes the change. Both of our Claude sessions follow the same playbook so we don't step on each other (pull-before-edit, append-don't-overwrite, one logical change per push).

**To get set up on your machine:**

```
cd ~/Downloads
git clone https://github.com/betterday-foodco/collab.git betterday-collab
```

Then any Claude session you start (in that folder or anywhere — Claude can navigate to it) will pick up the protocol automatically. First time, you can just say 'read HOW_TO_USE.md in the betterday-collab repo so you know how to update the board' and it'll be ready.

**What's waiting for you right now:**

- **task-001:** review PR #7 (feature/corp-manager-api in culinary-ops). Build passes clean, test plan checklist is in the PR body.
- **proj-001:** the full Flask→culinary-ops migration plan, 8 phases, so you can see what comes after PR #7 lands.

No rush on any of it — whenever you have time. Just wanted to make sure you knew the board exists, how the tabs work, and how to drive it through Claude instead of editing JSON by hand.

— Conner
